title: Market Brief — Sunday, June 7, 2026
summary: GPU availability is loosening fast across next-gen silicon: B200 util-avail sits at 58%, H200 at 53%, signaling emerging oversupply, while H100 remains the tightest class at 20% util-avail, approaching scarcity. The day-over-day price story screams panic buying into that H100 squeeze: B200 spot surged 48.5% to $6.24/hr, H200 jumped 27.2% to $3.82/hr, and H100 popped 18.1% to $2.36/hr, a violent repricing despite collapsing utilization everywhere else.
paragraphs: GPU availability is loosening fast across next-gen silicon: B200 util-avail sits at 58%, H200 at 53%, signaling emerging oversupply, while H100 remains the tightest class at 20% util-avail, approaching scarcity. The day-over-day price story screams panic buying into that H100 squeeze: B200 spot surged 48.5% to $6.24/hr, H200 jumped 27.2% to $3.82/hr, and H100 popped 18.1% to $2.36/hr, a violent repricing despite collapsing utilization everywhere else.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), serving Llama 70B on H100 runs roughly $8.64/M tokens and on H200 about $9.14/M, making medium-tier inference a cash incinerator at losses exceeding $5/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output is selling at a catastrophic loss against any hardware stack, grade: Burning Cash. Anthropic charges $25/M output for Opus 4.6, grade: Strong. OpenAI spans from $80/M premium o3 Pro to the $0.18/M gpt-oss-120b loss leader, grade: Neutral. Google holds margins on Gemini 2.5 Pro at $10/M output, grade: Neutral.

H100 at 20% util-avail with 76% annualized vol is overvalued on spot given the 14pp utilization drop in 30 days, and locking in contracts here means buying into a falling demand curve. B200 and H200 at 58% and 53% availability represent undervalued inference capacity, so the trade is shift Llama 70B workloads to B200 at $4.56/M raw cost where margins turn positive at high tier.
bullets: 58% util-avail on B200 and 53% on H200 confirm next-gen GPU oversupply is here.
20% util-avail on H100 makes it the only class near the scarcity threshold despite a 14.2pp utilization drop in 30 days.
48.5% single-day B200 spot surge to $6.24/hr is the largest move across all GPU classes.
21.78 LLMflation index means frontier inference costs have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.34/M output on DeepSeek V3.2 sits roughly $5.30/M below TCO-adjusted H100 production cost.
119 of 328 advertised A100 offerings sit idle, the tightest rental fill rate at 36% free across all classes.
